what_it_does: Defines a React HubSettings screen that renders editable hub metadata, menu/navigation option toggles, linked hub toggles, and two centered preview placeholders, then exposes the component on window.HubSettings.
observations: Uses React state to store boolean settings for menu options, navigation options, link hub options, and nested hub entries.
Defines a local Check component that renders a custom checkbox-like row and toggles the corresponding state key on click.
Renders Hub Name and Meta Description fields with default text values.
Shows a URL preview containing highlighted path segments and a lock emoji.
Uses inline CSS grid layouts and inline typography/layout styles alongside cv-* class names.
Includes two sunken preview panels with the same circular play SVG icon.
Some section headers include a chevron-like character and an info indicator span.
standouts: The settings are represented as a single local object with many boolean flags.
The custom Check rows are reused for flat and indented option groups.
The screen combines form fields, grouped toggles, helper copy, and preview placeholders in one component.
conventions: Uses a destructured React useState alias named useState_hs.
Attaches the screen component to the global window object.
Uses cv-* design-system classes for inputs, panels, labels, checks, and title text.
Relies heavily on inline style objects for screen-specific grid, spacing, preview, and text details.
Uses a locally defined component inside the parent component rather than a separate exported module.
concerns: Clickable check rows are div elements without native checkbox semantics or keyboard handling in this file.
The link variant renders an anchor without an href.
Several layout and preview style blocks are inline and repeated.
toggle reads from the current closure state rather than using a functional state update.
notes: This file is a self-contained Virtual Hub Settings screen with local toggle state and custom checkbox-style controls.
questions: Should the preview placeholders open or display an actual preview on double click?
Should the Floorplan label behave as a real navigation link?
Are the settings meant to persist outside local component state?
What behavior should the Apply to all Hubs option trigger?
Should the lock indicator in the URL preview be an icon instead of text content?
purpose_vs_actual: The component name and rendered title indicate a Virtual Hub Settings screen, and the fields, option groups, linked hub controls, and preview placeholders align with that purpose.
intention_signals: It appears intended to let a platform user configure the public-facing behavior and linked variations of a virtual hub while seeing preview entry points for menu and hub-link sections.
novelty: The file uses custom checkbox rows over a single local boolean map and includes repeated play-button preview placeholders directly inside the settings layout.
contribution: Provides a concrete settings-screen pattern for hub metadata editing, grouped boolean feature controls, nested linked-hub selection, helper copy, and preview-placeholder areas within a cv-* styled interface.
summary_for_embedding: A React screen component for Virtual Hub Settings with local boolean state, custom checkbox rows, hub name/meta fields, menu and navigation toggles, linked hub options, and preview placeholders.
intention_for_embedding: This was for configuring a virtual hub's metadata, visible menu/navigation features, and linked hub switching options in a platform settings UI.

symbols.does: Renders the Virtual Hub Settings screen and manages local boolean option state.
symbols.description: HubSettings initializes a local settings object, exposes a toggle helper, defines the Check row component, and returns the full settings UI. It includes hub metadata fields, grouped menu/navigation controls, linked hub controls, helper text, and preview placeholder panels before assigning itself to window.HubSettings.

symbols.does: Renders a custom checkbox-like option row bound to one key in HubSettings state.
symbols.description: Check receives a state key, label, and optional link flag, then displays a cv-check row with an off class when the value is false. Clicking the row toggles the associated setting, and the label is rendered either as an anchor-styled element or a span.
